values.summary: 英数 key on Mac keyboard.

values.name: KEY_JIS_KANA
values.summary: かな key on Mac keyboard.

values.name: KEY_KP_ADD
values.summary: Add (+) key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_KP_DIVIDE
values.summary: Divide (/) key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_KP_ENTER
values.summary: Enter key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_KP_MULTIPLY
values.summary: Multiply (*) key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_KP_PERIOD
values.summary: Period (.) key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_KP_SUBTRACT
values.summary: Subtract (-) key on the numeric keypad.

values.name: KEY_NONE
values.summary: Enum value which doesn't correspond to any key. This is used to initialize [enum Key] properties with a generic state.

values.name: KEY_SPECIAL
values.summary: Keycodes with this bit applied are non-printable.

values.name: KEY_LOCATION_LEFT
values.summary: A key which is to the left of its twin.

values.name: KEY_LOCATION_RIGHT
values.summary: A key which is to the right of its twin.

values.name: KEY_LOCATION_UNSPECIFIED
values.summary: Used for keys which only appear once, or when a comparison doesn't need to differentiate the [code]LEFT[/code] and [code]RIGHT[/code] versions. For example, when using [method InputEvent.is_match], an event which has [constant KEY_LOCATION_UNSPECIFIED] will match any [enum KeyLocation] on the passed event.

values.name: KEY_CODE_MASK
values.summary: Bit mask with all bits enabled except for modifier keys. Apply it to remove modifiers. [codeblock] var keycode = KEY_A | KEY_MASK_SHIFT keycode = keycode & KEY_CODE_MASK print(keycode) # KEY_A [/codeblock]

values.name: KEY_MASK_ALT
values.summary: Alt or Option (on macOS) key mask.

values.name: KEY_MASK_CMD_OR_CTRL
values.summary: Automatically remapped to [constant KEY_META] on macOS and [constant KEY_CTRL] on other platforms, this mask is never set in the actual events, and should be used for key mapping only.

values.name: KEY_MASK_META
values.summary: Command (on macOS) or Meta/Windows key mask.

values.name: KEY_MODIFIER_MASK
values.summary: Bit mask with all modifier bits enabled. Apply it to isolate modifiers. [codeblock] var keycode = KEY_A | KEY_MASK_SHIFT keycode = keycode & KEY_MODIFIER_MASK print(keycode) # KEY_MASK_SHIFT [/codeblock]

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_ACTIVE_SENSING
values.summary: MIDI message sent repeatedly while the MIDI device is idle, to tell the receiver that the connection is alive. Most MIDI devices do not send this message.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_AFTERTOUCH
values.summary: MIDI message sent to indicate a change in pressure while a note is being pressed down, also called aftertouch.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CHANNEL_PRESSURE
values.summary: MIDI message sent to indicate a change in pressure for the whole channel. Some MIDI devices may send this instead of [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_AFTERTOUCH].

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CONTINUE
values.summary: MIDI message sent to resume from the point the current sequence or song was paused.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CONTROL_CHANGE
values.summary: MIDI message sent when a controller value changes. In a MIDI device, a controller is any input that doesn't play notes. These may include sliders for volume, balance, and panning, as well as switches and pedals. See the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_MIDI#Controller_events]General MIDI specification[/url] for a small list.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NONE
values.summary: Does not correspond to any MIDI message. This is the default value of [member InputEventMIDI.message].

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_OFF
values.summary: MIDI message sent when a note is released. [b]Note:[/b] Not all MIDI devices send this message; some may send [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_ON] with [member InputEventMIDI.velocity] set to [code]0[/code].

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_ON
values.summary: MIDI message sent when a note is pressed.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_PITCH_BEND
values.summary: MIDI message sent when the value of the pitch bender changes, usually a wheel on the MIDI device.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_PROGRAM_CHANGE
values.summary: MIDI message sent when the MIDI device changes its current instrument (also called [i]program[/i] or [i]preset[/i]).

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_QUARTER_FRAME
values.summary: MIDI message sent every quarter frame to keep connected MIDI devices synchronized. Related to [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_TIMING_CLOCK]. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SONG_POSITION_POINTER
values.summary: MIDI message sent to jump onto a new position in the current sequence or song. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SONG_SELECT
values.summary: MIDI message sent to select a sequence or song to play. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_START
values.summary: MIDI message sent to start the current sequence or song from the beginning.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_STOP
values.summary: MIDI message sent to pause the current sequence or song.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SYSTEM_EXCLUSIVE
values.summary: MIDI system exclusive (SysEx) message. This type of message is not standardized and it's highly dependent on the MIDI device sending it. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SYSTEM_RESET
values.summary: MIDI message sent to reset a MIDI device to its default state, as if it was just turned on. It should not be sent when the MIDI device is being turned on.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_TIMING_CLOCK
values.summary: MIDI message sent 24 times after [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_QUARTER_FRAME], to keep connected MIDI devices synchronized.

values.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_TUNE_REQUEST
values.summary: MIDI message sent to request a tuning calibration. Used on analog synthesizers. Most modern MIDI devices do not need this message.

values.name: METHOD_FLAG_OBJECT_CORE
values.summary: Used internally. Allows to not dump core virtual methods (such as [method Object._notification]) to the JSON API.

values.name: METHOD_FLAG_VARARG
values.summary: Flag for a method with a variable number of arguments.

values.name: METHOD_FLAG_VIRTUAL_REQUIRED
values.summary: Flag for a virtual method that is required. In GDScript, this flag is set for abstract functions.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT
values.summary: Primary mouse button, usually assigned to the left button.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_NONE
values.summary: Enum value which doesn't correspond to any mouse button. This is used to initialize [enum MouseButton] properties with a generic state.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_RIGHT
values.summary: Secondary mouse button, usually assigned to the right button.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_WHEEL_LEFT
values.summary: Mouse wheel left button (only present on some mice).

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_WHEEL_RIGHT
values.summary: Mouse wheel right button (only present on some mice).

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_XBUTTON1
values.summary: Extra mouse button 1. This is sometimes present, usually to the sides of the mouse.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_XBUTTON2
values.summary: Extra mouse button 2. This is sometimes present, usually to the sides of the mouse.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_LEFT
values.summary: Primary mouse button mask, usually for the left button.

values.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_RIGHT
values.summary: Secondary mouse button mask, usually for the right button.

values.name: HORIZONTAL
values.summary: General horizontal alignment, usually used for [Separator], [ScrollBar], [Slider], etc.

values.name: VERTICAL
values.summary: General vertical alignment, usually used for [Separator], [ScrollBar], [Slider], etc.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE
values.summary: Hints that a property is an [Array] with the stored type specified in the hint string. The hint string contains the type of the array (e.g. [code]"String"[/code]). Use the hint string format from [constant PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING] for more control over the stored type.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_COLOR_NO_ALPHA
values.summary: Hints that a [Color] property should be edited without affecting its transparency ([member Color.a] is not editable).

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_DICTIONARY_TYPE
values.summary: Hints that a property is a [Dictionary] with the stored types specified in the hint string. The hint string contains the key and value types separated by a semicolon (e.g. [code]"int;String"[/code]). Use the hint string format from [constant PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING] for more control over the stored types.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_DIR
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a directory. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM
values.summary: Hints that an [int], [String], or [StringName] property is an enumerated value to pick in a list specified via a hint string. The hint string is a comma separated list of names such as [code]"Hello,Something,Else"[/code]. Whitespace is [b]not[/b] removed from either end of a name. For integer properties, the first name in the list has value 0, the next 1, and so on. Explicit values can also be specified by appending [code]:integer[/code] to the name, e.g. [code]"Zero,One,Three:3,Four,Six:6"[/code].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM_SUGGESTION
values.summary: Hints that a [String] or [StringName] property can be an enumerated value to pick in a list specified via a hint string such as [code]"Hello,Something,Else"[/code]. See [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] for details. Unlike [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM], a property with this hint still accepts arbitrary values and can be empty. The list of values serves to suggest possible values.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_EXPRESSION
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an [Expression].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_EXP_EASING
values.summary: Hints that a [float] property should be edited using a curve editor showing an exponential easing function. The hint string can include [code]"attenuation"[/code] to flip the curve horizontally and/or [code]"positive_only"[/code] to exclude in/out easing and limit values to be greater than or equal to zero. This displays differently to a property that uses [constant PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] with the [code]"exp"[/code] keyword, as it's edited with a slider instead of a curve editor.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FILE
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. By default the file will be stored as UID whenever available. You can use [ResourceUID] methods to convert it back to path. For storing a raw path, use [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE_PATH].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FILE_PATH
values.summary: Like [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE], but the property is stored as a raw path, not UID. That means the reference will be broken if you move the file. Consider using [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE] when possible.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FLAGS
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask with named bit flags. The hint string is a comma separated list of names such as [code]"Bit0,Bit1,Bit2,Bit3"[/code]. Whitespace is [b]not[/b] removed from either end of a name. The first name in the list has value 1, the next 2, then 4, 8, 16 and so on. Explicit values can also be specified by appending [code]:integer[/code] to the name, e.g. [code]"A:4,B:8,C:16"[/code]. You can also combine several flags ([code]"A:4,B:8,AB:12,C:16"[/code]). [b]Note:[/b] A flag value must be at least [code]1[/code] and at most [code]2 ** 32 - 1[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM], the previous explicit value is not taken into account. For the hint [code]"A:16,B,C"[/code], A is 16, B is 2, C is 4.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_DIR
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an absolute path to a directory outside the project folder. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_FILE
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an absolute path to a file outside the project folder. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards, like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_SAVE_FILE
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path for the file to be saved at. The dialog has access to the entire filesystem. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. See also [member FileDialog.filters].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE
values.summary: Hints that a boolean property will enable the feature associated with the group that it occurs in. The property will be displayed as a checkbox on the group header. Only works within a group or subgroup. By default, disabling the property hides all properties in the group. Use the optional hint string [code]"checkbox_only"[/code] to disable this behavior.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_HIDE_QUATERNION_EDIT
values.summary: Hints that a quaternion property should disable the temporary euler editor.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_INPUT_NAME
values.summary: Hints that a [String] or [StringName] property is the name of an input action. This allows the selection of any action name from the Input Map in the Project Settings. The hint string may contain two options separated by commas: - If it contains [code]"show_builtin"[/code], built-in input actions are included in the selection. - If it contains [code]"loose_mode"[/code], loose mode is enabled. This allows inserting any action name even if it's not present in the input map.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_INT_IS_POINTER
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a pointer. Used by GDExtension.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_NAVIGATION
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D navigation layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_PHYSICS
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D physics layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_RENDER
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D render layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_NAVIGATION
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D navigation layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_PHYSICS
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D physics layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_RENDER
values.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D render layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_AVOIDANCE
values.summary: Hints that an integer property is a bitmask using the optionally named avoidance layers.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LINK
values.summary: Hints that a vector property should allow its components to be linked. For example, this allows [member Vector2.x] and [member Vector2.y] to be edited together. This hint is supported on [Vector2], [Vector2i], [Vector3], [Vector3i], [Vector4], and [Vector4i]. The hint string can be used to specify a suffix indicating each value's unit with the [code]"suffix:px/s"[/code] syntax.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LOCALE_ID
values.summary: Hints that a string property is a locale code. Editing it will show a locale dialog for picking language and country.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LOCALIZABLE_STRING
values.summary: Hints that a dictionary property is string translation map. Dictionary keys are locale codes and, values are translated strings.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PropertyHint] enum.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_MULTILINE_TEXT
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is text with line breaks. Editing it will show a text input field where line breaks can be typed. The hint string can be set to [code]"monospace"[/code] to force the input field to use a monospaced font. If the hint string [code]"no_wrap"[/code] is set, the input field will not wrap lines at boundaries, instead resorting to making the area scrollable.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NODE_PATH_VALID_TYPES
values.summary: Hints that the hint string specifies valid node types for property of type [NodePath].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NODE_TYPE
values.summary: Hints that a property is an instance of a [Node]-derived type, optionally specified via the hint string (e.g. [code]"Node2D"[/code]). Editing it will show a dialog for picking a node from the scene.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NONE
values.summary: The property has no hint for the editor. However, the hint string is still read, which can be used to specify a suffix for a property that has no range limit (see [constant PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE]'s description).

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_OBJECT_ID
values.summary: Hints that the property's value is an object encoded as object ID, with its type specified in the hint string. Used by the debugger.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_OBJECT_TOO_BIG
values.summary: Hints that an object is too big to be sent via the debugger.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ONESHOT
values.summary: Hints that a property will be changed on its own after setting, such as [member AudioStreamPlayer.playing] or [member GPUParticles3D.emitting].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_PASSWORD
values.summary: Hints that a string property is a password, and every character is replaced with the secret character.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_PLACEHOLDER_TEXT
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property should show a placeholder text on its input field, if empty. The hint string is the placeholder text to use.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE
values.summary: Hints that an [int], [float], or packed/typed [Array] property containing [int] or [float] types should be within a range specified via the hint string [code]"min,max"[/code] or [code]"min,max,step"[/code]. The hint string can optionally include [code]"or_greater"[/code] and/or [code]"or_less"[/code] to allow manual input going respectively above the max or below the min values. [b]Example:[/b] [code]"-360,360,1,or_greater,or_less"[/code]. Additionally, other keywords can be included: [code]"exp"[/code] for exponential range editing, [code]"radians_as_degrees"[/code] for editing radian angles in degrees (the range values are also in degrees), [code]"degrees"[/code] to hint at an angle, [code]"prefer_slider"[/code] to show the slider for integers, [code]"hide_control"[/code] to hide the slider or up-down arrows, and [code]"suffix:px/s"[/code] to display a suffix indicating the value's unit (e.g. [code]px/s[/code] for pixels per second).

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE
values.summary: Hints that a property is an instance of a [Resource]-derived type, optionally specified via the hint string (e.g. [code]"Texture2D"[/code]). Editing it will show a popup menu of valid resource types to instantiate.

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_SAVE_FILE
values.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path for the file to be saved at. The dialog has access to the project's directory. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. See also [member FileDialog.filters].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_TOOL_BUTTON
values.summary: Hints that a [Callable] property should be displayed as a clickable button. When the button is pressed, the callable is called. The hint string specifies the button text and optionally an icon from the [code]"EditorIcons"[/code] theme type. [codeblock lang=text] "Click me!" - A button with the text "Click me!" and the default "Callable" icon. "Click me!,ColorRect" - A button with the text "Click me!" and the "ColorRect" icon. [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] A [Callable] cannot be properly serialized and stored in a file, so it is recommended to use [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR] instead of [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_DEFAULT].

values.name: PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING
values.summary: If a property is [String], hints that the property represents a particular type (class). This allows to select a type from the create dialog. The property will store the selected type as a string. If a property is [Array], hints the editor how to show elements. The [code]hint_string[/code] must encode nested types using [code]":"[/code] and [code]"/"[/code]. If a property is [Dictionary], hints the editor how to show elements. The [code]hint_string[/code] is the same as [Array], with a [code]";"[/code] separating the key and value. [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Array of elem_type. hint_string = "%d:" % [elem_type] hint_string = "%d/%d:%s" % [elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] # Two-dimensional array of elem_type (array of arrays of elem_type). hint_string = "%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type] hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:%s" % [TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] # Three-dimensional array of elem_type (array of arrays of arrays of elem_type). hint_string = "%d:%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type] hint_string = "%d:%d:%d/%d:%s" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] [/gdscript] [csharp] // Array of elemType. hintString = $"{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; // Two-dimensional array of elemType (array of arrays of elemType). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; // Three-dimensional array of elemType (array of arrays of arrays of elemType). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Examples:[/b] [codeblocks] [gdscript] hint_string = "%d:" % [TYPE_INT] # Array of integers. hint_string = "%d/%d:1,10,1" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] # Array of integers (in range from 1 to 10). hint_string = "%d/%d:Zero,One,Two" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] # Array of integers (an enum). hint_string = "%d/%d:Zero,One,Three:3,Six:6" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] # Array of integers (an enum). hint_string = "%d/%d:*.png" % [TYPE_STRING, PROPERTY_HINT_FILE] # Array of strings (file paths). hint_string = "%d/%d:Texture2D" % [TYPE_OBJECT, PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE] # Array of textures. hint_string = "%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_FLOAT] # Two-dimensional array of floats. hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_STRING, PROPERTY_HINT_MULTILINE_TEXT] # Two-dimensional array of multiline strings. hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:-1,1,0.1" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_FLOAT, PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] # Two-dimensional array of floats (in range from -1 to 1). hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:Texture2D" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_OBJECT, PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE] # Two-dimensional array of textures. [/gdscript] [csharp] hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Range:D}:1,10,1"; // Array of integers (in range from 1 to 10). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Enum:D}:Zero,One,Two"; // Array of integers (an enum). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Enum:D}:Zero,One,Three:3,Six:6"; // Array of integers (an enum). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.String:D}/{PropertyHint.File:D}:*.png"; // Array of strings (file paths). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Object:D}/{PropertyHint.ResourceType:D}:Texture2D"; // Array of textures. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Float:D}:"; // Two-dimensional array of floats. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.String:D}/{PropertyHint.MultilineText:D}:"; // Two-dimensional array of multiline strings. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Float:D}/{PropertyHint.Range:D}:-1,1,0.1"; // Two-dimensional array of floats (in range from -1 to 1). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Object:D}/{PropertyHint.ResourceType:D}:Texture2D"; // Two-dimensional array of textures. [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] The trailing colon is required for properly detecting built-in types.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_ALWAYS_DUPLICATE
values.summary: When duplicating a resource with [method Resource.duplicate], and this flag is set on a property of that resource, the property should always be duplicated, regardless of the [code]subresources[/code] bool parameter.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_ARRAY
values.summary: The property is the element count of a property array, i.e. a list of groups of related properties. Properties defined with this usage also need a specific [code]class_name[/code] field in the form of [code]label,prefix[/code]. The field may also include additional comma-separated options: - [code]page_size=N[/code]: Overrides [member EditorSettings.interface/inspector/max_array_dictionary_items_per_page] for this array. - [code]add_button_text=text[/code]: The text displayed by the "Add Element" button. - [code]static[/code]: The elements can't be re-arranged. - [code]const[/code]: New elements can't be added. - [code]numbered[/code]: An index will appear next to each element. - [code]unfoldable[/code]: The array can't be folded. - [code]swap_method=method_name[/code]: The method that will be called when two elements switch places. The method should take 2 [int] parameters, which will be indices of the elements being swapped. Note that making a full-fledged property array requires boilerplate code involving [method Object._get_property_list].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CATEGORY
values.summary: Used to categorize properties together in the editor.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CHECKABLE
values.summary: The property can be checked in the [EditorInspector].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CHECKED
values.summary: The property is checked in the [EditorInspector].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CLASS_IS_BITFIELD
values.summary: The property is a bitfield, i.e. it contains multiple flags represented as bits.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CLASS_IS_ENUM
values.summary: The property is a variable of enum type, i.e. it only takes named integer constants from its associated enumeration.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR
values.summary: The property is shown in the [EditorInspector] (default for exported properties).

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR_BASIC_SETTING
values.summary: The property is considered a basic setting and will appear even when advanced mode is disabled. Used for project settings.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR_INSTANTIATE_OBJECT
values.summary: When this property is a [Resource] and base object is a [Node], a resource instance will be automatically created whenever the node is created in the editor.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_GROUP
values.summary: Used to group properties together in the editor. See [EditorInspector].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_HIGH_END_GFX
values.summary: The property is only shown in the editor if modern renderers are supported (the Compatibility rendering method is excluded).

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_INTERNAL
values.summary: The property is excluded from the class reference.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_KEYING_INCREMENTS
values.summary: Inserting an animation key frame of this property will automatically increment the value, allowing to easily keyframe multiple values in a row.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NEVER_DUPLICATE
values.summary: When duplicating a resource with [method Resource.duplicate], and this flag is set on a property of that resource, the property should never be duplicated, regardless of the [code]subresources[/code] bool parameter.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NIL_IS_VARIANT
values.summary: If property has [code]nil[/code] as default value, its type will be [Variant].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NODE_PATH_FROM_SCENE_ROOT
values.summary: The [NodePath] property will always be relative to the scene's root. Mostly useful for local resources.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NONE
values.summary: The property is not stored, and does not display in the editor. This is the default for non-exported properties.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NO_EDITOR
values.summary: Default usage but without showing the property in the editor (storage).

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NO_INSTANCE_STATE
values.summary: The property does not save its state in [PackedScene].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_READ_ONLY
values.summary: The property is read-only in the [EditorInspector].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_RESOURCE_NOT_PERSISTENT
values.summary: Use when a resource is created on the fly, i.e. the getter will always return a different instance. [ResourceSaver] needs this information to properly save such resources.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_RESTART_IF_CHANGED
values.summary: Editing the property prompts the user for restarting the editor.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE
values.summary: The property is a script variable. [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE] can be used to distinguish between exported script variables from built-in variables (which don't have this usage flag). By default, [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE] is [b]not[/b] applied to variables that are created by overriding [method Object._get_property_list] in a script.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SECRET
values.summary: An export preset property with this flag contains confidential information and is stored separately from the rest of the export preset configuration.

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_STORAGE
values.summary: The property is serialized and saved in the scene file (default for exported properties).

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_STORE_IF_NULL
values.summary: The property value of type [Object] will be stored even if its value is [code]null[/code].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SUBGROUP
values.summary: Used to group properties together in the editor in a subgroup (under a group). See [EditorInspector].

values.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_UPDATE_ALL_IF_MODIFIED
values.summary: If this property is modified, all inspector fields will be refreshed.

values.name: SIDE_BOTTOM
values.summary: Bottom side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

values.name: SIDE_LEFT
values.summary: Left side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

values.name: SIDE_RIGHT
values.summary: Right side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

values.name: SIDE_TOP
values.summary: Top side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

values.name: OP_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Variant.Operator] enum.

values.name: OP_XOR
values.summary: Logical XOR operator (not implemented in GDScript).

values.name: TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Variant.Type] enum.

values.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_BOTTOM
values.summary: Vertical bottom alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

values.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER
values.summary: Vertical center alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

values.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_FILL
values.summary: Expand rows to fit height, usually for text-derived classes.

values.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_TOP
values.summary: Vertical top alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: SIDE_LEFT
constants.summary: Left side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

constants.name: SIDE_TOP
constants.summary: Top side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

constants.name: SIDE_RIGHT
constants.summary: Right side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

constants.name: SIDE_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Bottom side, usually used for [Control] or [StyleBox]-derived classes.

constants.name: VERTICAL
constants.summary: General vertical alignment, usually used for [Separator], [ScrollBar], [Slider], etc.

constants.name: HORIZONTAL
constants.summary: General horizontal alignment, usually used for [Separator], [ScrollBar], [Slider], etc.

constants.name: CLOCKWISE
constants.summary: Clockwise rotation. Used by some methods (e.g. [method Image.rotate_90]).

constants.name: COUNTERCLOCKWISE
constants.summary: Counter-clockwise rotation. Used by some methods (e.g. [method Image.rotate_90]).

constants.name: HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_LEFT
constants.summary: Horizontal left alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER
constants.summary: Horizontal center alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_RIGHT
constants.summary: Horizontal right alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: HORIZONTAL_ALIGNMENT_FILL
constants.summary: Expand row to fit width, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_TOP
constants.summary: Vertical top alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_CENTER
constants.summary: Vertical center alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Vertical bottom alignment, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: VERTICAL_ALIGNMENT_FILL
constants.summary: Expand rows to fit height, usually for text-derived classes.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TOP_TO
constants.summary: Aligns the top of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the position of the text specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_*[/code] constant.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_CENTER_TO
constants.summary: Aligns the center of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the position of the text specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_*[/code] constant.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_BASELINE_TO
constants.summary: Aligns the baseline (user defined) of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the position of the text specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_*[/code] constant.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_BOTTOM_TO
constants.summary: Aligns the bottom of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the position of the text specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_*[/code] constant.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_TOP
constants.summary: Aligns the position of the inline object (e.g. image, table) specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_*_TO[/code] constant to the top of the text.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_CENTER
constants.summary: Aligns the position of the inline object (e.g. image, table) specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_*_TO[/code] constant to the center of the text.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_BASELINE
constants.summary: Aligns the position of the inline object (e.g. image, table) specified by [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_*_TO[/code] constant to the baseline of the text.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Aligns inline object (e.g. image, table) to the bottom of the text.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TOP
constants.summary: Aligns top of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the top of the text. Equivalent to [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TOP_TO | INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_TOP[/code].

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_CENTER
constants.summary: Aligns center of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the center of the text. Equivalent to [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_CENTER_TO | INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_CENTER[/code].

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_BOTTOM
constants.summary: Aligns bottom of the inline object (e.g. image, table) to the bottom of the text. Equivalent to [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_BOTTOM_TO | INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_BOTTOM[/code].

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_IMAGE_MASK
constants.summary: A bit mask for [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_*_TO[/code] alignment constants.

constants.name: INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TEXT_MASK
constants.summary: A bit mask for [code]INLINE_ALIGNMENT_TO_*[/code] alignment constants.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_XYZ
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic XYZ order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local X, Y, and Z axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first Z, then Y, and X last.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_XZY
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic XZY order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local X, Z, and Y axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first Y, then Z, and X last.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_YXZ
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic YXZ order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local Y, X, and Z axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first Z, then X, and Y last.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_YZX
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic YZX order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local Y, Z, and X axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first X, then Z, and Y last.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_ZXY
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic ZXY order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local Z, X, and Y axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first Y, then X, and Z last.

constants.name: EULER_ORDER_ZYX
constants.summary: Specifies that Euler angles should be in intrinsic ZYX order. When composing, the rotations happen around the local Z, Y, and X axes, in that order. When decomposing, the order is reversed, first X, then Y, and Z last.

constants.name: KEY_NONE
constants.summary: Enum value which doesn't correspond to any key. This is used to initialize [enum Key] properties with a generic state.

constants.name: KEY_SPECIAL
constants.summary: Keycodes with this bit applied are non-printable.

constants.name: KEY_KP_ENTER
constants.summary: Enter key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_F25
constants.summary: F25 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F26
constants.summary: F26 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F27
constants.summary: F27 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F28
constants.summary: F28 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F29
constants.summary: F29 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F30
constants.summary: F30 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F31
constants.summary: F31 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F32
constants.summary: F32 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F33
constants.summary: F33 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F34
constants.summary: F34 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_F35
constants.summary: F35 key. Only supported on macOS and Linux due to a Windows limitation.

constants.name: KEY_KP_MULTIPLY
constants.summary: Multiply (*) key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_KP_DIVIDE
constants.summary: Divide (/) key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_KP_SUBTRACT
constants.summary: Subtract (-) key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_KP_PERIOD
constants.summary: Period (.) key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_KP_ADD
constants.summary: Add (+) key on the numeric keypad.

constants.name: KEY_JIS_EISU
constants.summary: 英数 key on Mac keyboard.

constants.name: KEY_JIS_KANA
constants.summary: かな key on Mac keyboard.

constants.name: KEY_CODE_MASK
constants.summary: Bit mask with all bits enabled except for modifier keys. Apply it to remove modifiers. [codeblock] var keycode = KEY_A | KEY_MASK_SHIFT keycode = keycode & KEY_CODE_MASK print(keycode) # KEY_A [/codeblock]

constants.name: KEY_MODIFIER_MASK
constants.summary: Bit mask with all modifier bits enabled. Apply it to isolate modifiers. [codeblock] var keycode = KEY_A | KEY_MASK_SHIFT keycode = keycode & KEY_MODIFIER_MASK print(keycode) # KEY_MASK_SHIFT [/codeblock]

constants.name: KEY_MASK_CMD_OR_CTRL
constants.summary: Automatically remapped to [constant KEY_META] on macOS and [constant KEY_CTRL] on other platforms, this mask is never set in the actual events, and should be used for key mapping only.

constants.name: KEY_MASK_ALT
constants.summary: Alt or Option (on macOS) key mask.

constants.name: KEY_MASK_META
constants.summary: Command (on macOS) or Meta/Windows key mask.

constants.name: KEY_LOCATION_UNSPECIFIED
constants.summary: Used for keys which only appear once, or when a comparison doesn't need to differentiate the [code]LEFT[/code] and [code]RIGHT[/code] versions. For example, when using [method InputEvent.is_match], an event which has [constant KEY_LOCATION_UNSPECIFIED] will match any [enum KeyLocation] on the passed event.

constants.name: KEY_LOCATION_LEFT
constants.summary: A key which is to the left of its twin.

constants.name: KEY_LOCATION_RIGHT
constants.summary: A key which is to the right of its twin.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_NONE
constants.summary: Enum value which doesn't correspond to any mouse button. This is used to initialize [enum MouseButton] properties with a generic state.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_LEFT
constants.summary: Primary mouse button, usually assigned to the left button.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_RIGHT
constants.summary: Secondary mouse button, usually assigned to the right button.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_WHEEL_LEFT
constants.summary: Mouse wheel left button (only present on some mice).

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_WHEEL_RIGHT
constants.summary: Mouse wheel right button (only present on some mice).

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_XBUTTON1
constants.summary: Extra mouse button 1. This is sometimes present, usually to the sides of the mouse.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_XBUTTON2
constants.summary: Extra mouse button 2. This is sometimes present, usually to the sides of the mouse.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_LEFT
constants.summary: Primary mouse button mask, usually for the left button.

constants.name: MOUSE_BUTTON_MASK_RIGHT
constants.summary: Secondary mouse button mask, usually for the right button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_A
constants.summary: Game controller SDL button A. Corresponds to the bottom action button: Sony Cross, Xbox A, Nintendo B.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_B
constants.summary: Game controller SDL button B. Corresponds to the right action button: Sony Circle, Xbox B, Nintendo A.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_X
constants.summary: Game controller SDL button X. Corresponds to the left action button: Sony Square, Xbox X, Nintendo Y.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_Y
constants.summary: Game controller SDL button Y. Corresponds to the top action button: Sony Triangle, Xbox Y, Nintendo X.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_BACK
constants.summary: Game controller SDL back button. Corresponds to the Sony Select, Xbox Back, Nintendo - button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_GUIDE
constants.summary: Game controller SDL guide button. Corresponds to the Sony PS, Xbox Home button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_START
constants.summary: Game controller SDL start button. Corresponds to the Sony Options, Xbox Menu, Nintendo + button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_LEFT_STICK
constants.summary: Game controller SDL left stick button. Corresponds to the Sony L3, Xbox L/LS button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_RIGHT_STICK
constants.summary: Game controller SDL right stick button. Corresponds to the Sony R3, Xbox R/RS button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_LEFT_SHOULDER
constants.summary: Game controller SDL left shoulder button. Corresponds to the Sony L1, Xbox LB button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_RIGHT_SHOULDER
constants.summary: Game controller SDL right shoulder button. Corresponds to the Sony R1, Xbox RB button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_MISC1
constants.summary: Game controller SDL miscellaneous button. Corresponds to Xbox share button, PS5 microphone button, Nintendo Switch capture button.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_MISC2
constants.summary: Game controller SDL miscellaneous button. Used by Nintendo Switch 2 Pro Controller and Horipad Steam controllers.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_SDL_MAX
constants.summary: The number of SDL game controller buttons.

constants.name: JOY_BUTTON_MAX
constants.summary: The maximum number of game controller buttons supported by the engine. The actual limit may be lower on specific platforms: - [b]Android:[/b] Up to 36 buttons. - [b]Linux:[/b] Up to 80 buttons. - [b]Windows[/b] and [b]macOS:[/b] Up to 128 buttons.

constants.name: JOY_AXIS_SDL_MAX
constants.summary: The number of SDL game controller axes.

constants.name: JOY_AXIS_MAX
constants.summary: The maximum number of game controller axes: OpenVR supports up to 5 Joysticks making a total of 10 axes.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NONE
constants.summary: Does not correspond to any MIDI message. This is the default value of [member InputEventMIDI.message].

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_OFF
constants.summary: MIDI message sent when a note is released. [b]Note:[/b] Not all MIDI devices send this message; some may send [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_ON] with [member InputEventMIDI.velocity] set to [code]0[/code].

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_NOTE_ON
constants.summary: MIDI message sent when a note is pressed.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_AFTERTOUCH
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to indicate a change in pressure while a note is being pressed down, also called aftertouch.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CONTROL_CHANGE
constants.summary: MIDI message sent when a controller value changes. In a MIDI device, a controller is any input that doesn't play notes. These may include sliders for volume, balance, and panning, as well as switches and pedals. See the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_MIDI#Controller_events]General MIDI specification[/url] for a small list.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_PROGRAM_CHANGE
constants.summary: MIDI message sent when the MIDI device changes its current instrument (also called [i]program[/i] or [i]preset[/i]).

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CHANNEL_PRESSURE
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to indicate a change in pressure for the whole channel. Some MIDI devices may send this instead of [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_AFTERTOUCH].

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_PITCH_BEND
constants.summary: MIDI message sent when the value of the pitch bender changes, usually a wheel on the MIDI device.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SYSTEM_EXCLUSIVE
constants.summary: MIDI system exclusive (SysEx) message. This type of message is not standardized and it's highly dependent on the MIDI device sending it. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_QUARTER_FRAME
constants.summary: MIDI message sent every quarter frame to keep connected MIDI devices synchronized. Related to [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_TIMING_CLOCK]. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SONG_POSITION_POINTER
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to jump onto a new position in the current sequence or song. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SONG_SELECT
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to select a sequence or song to play. [b]Note:[/b] Getting this message's data from [InputEventMIDI] is not implemented.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_TUNE_REQUEST
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to request a tuning calibration. Used on analog synthesizers. Most modern MIDI devices do not need this message.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_TIMING_CLOCK
constants.summary: MIDI message sent 24 times after [constant MIDI_MESSAGE_QUARTER_FRAME], to keep connected MIDI devices synchronized.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_START
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to start the current sequence or song from the beginning.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_CONTINUE
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to resume from the point the current sequence or song was paused.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_STOP
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to pause the current sequence or song.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_ACTIVE_SENSING
constants.summary: MIDI message sent repeatedly while the MIDI device is idle, to tell the receiver that the connection is alive. Most MIDI devices do not send this message.

constants.name: MIDI_MESSAGE_SYSTEM_RESET
constants.summary: MIDI message sent to reset a MIDI device to its default state, as if it was just turned on. It should not be sent when the MIDI device is being turned on.

constants.name: OK
constants.summary: Methods that return [enum Error] return [constant OK] when no error occurred. Since [constant OK] has value [code]0[/code], and all other error constants are positive integers, it can also be used in boolean checks. [codeblock] var error = method_that_returns_error() if error != OK: printerr("Failure!") # Or, alternatively: if error: printerr("Still failing!") [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] Many functions do not return an error code, but will print error messages to standard output.

constants.name: ERR_HELP
constants.summary: Help error. Used internally when passing [code]--version[/code] or [code]--help[/code] as executable options.

constants.name: ERR_BUG
constants.summary: Bug error, caused by an implementation issue in the method. [b]Note:[/b] If a built-in method returns this code, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues]the GitHub Issue Tracker[/url].

constants.name: ERR_PRINTER_ON_FIRE
constants.summary: Printer on fire error (this is an easter egg, no built-in methods return this error code).

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NONE
constants.summary: The property has no hint for the editor. However, the hint string is still read, which can be used to specify a suffix for a property that has no range limit (see [constant PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE]'s description).

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE
constants.summary: Hints that an [int], [float], or packed/typed [Array] property containing [int] or [float] types should be within a range specified via the hint string [code]"min,max"[/code] or [code]"min,max,step"[/code]. The hint string can optionally include [code]"or_greater"[/code] and/or [code]"or_less"[/code] to allow manual input going respectively above the max or below the min values. [b]Example:[/b] [code]"-360,360,1,or_greater,or_less"[/code]. Additionally, other keywords can be included: [code]"exp"[/code] for exponential range editing, [code]"radians_as_degrees"[/code] for editing radian angles in degrees (the range values are also in degrees), [code]"degrees"[/code] to hint at an angle, [code]"prefer_slider"[/code] to show the slider for integers, [code]"hide_control"[/code] to hide the slider or up-down arrows, and [code]"suffix:px/s"[/code] to display a suffix indicating the value's unit (e.g. [code]px/s[/code] for pixels per second).

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM
constants.summary: Hints that an [int], [String], or [StringName] property is an enumerated value to pick in a list specified via a hint string. The hint string is a comma separated list of names such as [code]"Hello,Something,Else"[/code]. Whitespace is [b]not[/b] removed from either end of a name. For integer properties, the first name in the list has value 0, the next 1, and so on. Explicit values can also be specified by appending [code]:integer[/code] to the name, e.g. [code]"Zero,One,Three:3,Four,Six:6"[/code].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM_SUGGESTION
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] or [StringName] property can be an enumerated value to pick in a list specified via a hint string such as [code]"Hello,Something,Else"[/code]. See [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] for details. Unlike [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM], a property with this hint still accepts arbitrary values and can be empty. The list of values serves to suggest possible values.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_EXP_EASING
constants.summary: Hints that a [float] property should be edited using a curve editor showing an exponential easing function. The hint string can include [code]"attenuation"[/code] to flip the curve horizontally and/or [code]"positive_only"[/code] to exclude in/out easing and limit values to be greater than or equal to zero. This displays differently to a property that uses [constant PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] with the [code]"exp"[/code] keyword, as it's edited with a slider instead of a curve editor.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LINK
constants.summary: Hints that a vector property should allow its components to be linked. For example, this allows [member Vector2.x] and [member Vector2.y] to be edited together. This hint is supported on [Vector2], [Vector2i], [Vector3], [Vector3i], [Vector4], and [Vector4i]. The hint string can be used to specify a suffix indicating each value's unit with the [code]"suffix:px/s"[/code] syntax.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FLAGS
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask with named bit flags. The hint string is a comma separated list of names such as [code]"Bit0,Bit1,Bit2,Bit3"[/code]. Whitespace is [b]not[/b] removed from either end of a name. The first name in the list has value 1, the next 2, then 4, 8, 16 and so on. Explicit values can also be specified by appending [code]:integer[/code] to the name, e.g. [code]"A:4,B:8,C:16"[/code]. You can also combine several flags ([code]"A:4,B:8,AB:12,C:16"[/code]). [b]Note:[/b] A flag value must be at least [code]1[/code] and at most [code]2 ** 32 - 1[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike [constant PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM], the previous explicit value is not taken into account. For the hint [code]"A:16,B,C"[/code], A is 16, B is 2, C is 4.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_RENDER
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D render layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_PHYSICS
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D physics layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_2D_NAVIGATION
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 2D navigation layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_RENDER
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D render layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_PHYSICS
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D physics layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_3D_NAVIGATION
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a bitmask using the optionally named 3D navigation layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LAYERS_AVOIDANCE
constants.summary: Hints that an integer property is a bitmask using the optionally named avoidance layers.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FILE
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. By default the file will be stored as UID whenever available. You can use [ResourceUID] methods to convert it back to path. For storing a raw path, use [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE_PATH].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_DIR
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a directory. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_FILE
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an absolute path to a file outside the project folder. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards, like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_DIR
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an absolute path to a directory outside the project folder. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE
constants.summary: Hints that a property is an instance of a [Resource]-derived type, optionally specified via the hint string (e.g. [code]"Texture2D"[/code]). Editing it will show a popup menu of valid resource types to instantiate.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_MULTILINE_TEXT
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is text with line breaks. Editing it will show a text input field where line breaks can be typed. The hint string can be set to [code]"monospace"[/code] to force the input field to use a monospaced font. If the hint string [code]"no_wrap"[/code] is set, the input field will not wrap lines at boundaries, instead resorting to making the area scrollable.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_EXPRESSION
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is an [Expression].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_PLACEHOLDER_TEXT
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property should show a placeholder text on its input field, if empty. The hint string is the placeholder text to use.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_COLOR_NO_ALPHA
constants.summary: Hints that a [Color] property should be edited without affecting its transparency ([member Color.a] is not editable).

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_OBJECT_ID
constants.summary: Hints that the property's value is an object encoded as object ID, with its type specified in the hint string. Used by the debugger.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING
constants.summary: If a property is [String], hints that the property represents a particular type (class). This allows to select a type from the create dialog. The property will store the selected type as a string. If a property is [Array], hints the editor how to show elements. The [code]hint_string[/code] must encode nested types using [code]":"[/code] and [code]"/"[/code]. If a property is [Dictionary], hints the editor how to show elements. The [code]hint_string[/code] is the same as [Array], with a [code]";"[/code] separating the key and value. [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Array of elem_type. hint_string = "%d:" % [elem_type] hint_string = "%d/%d:%s" % [elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] # Two-dimensional array of elem_type (array of arrays of elem_type). hint_string = "%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type] hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:%s" % [TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] # Three-dimensional array of elem_type (array of arrays of arrays of elem_type). hint_string = "%d:%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type] hint_string = "%d:%d:%d/%d:%s" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_ARRAY, elem_type, elem_hint, elem_hint_string] [/gdscript] [csharp] // Array of elemType. hintString = $"{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; // Two-dimensional array of elemType (array of arrays of elemType). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; // Three-dimensional array of elemType (array of arrays of arrays of elemType). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}:"; hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{elemType:D}/{elemHint:D}:{elemHintString}"; [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Examples:[/b] [codeblocks] [gdscript] hint_string = "%d:" % [TYPE_INT] # Array of integers. hint_string = "%d/%d:1,10,1" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] # Array of integers (in range from 1 to 10). hint_string = "%d/%d:Zero,One,Two" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] # Array of integers (an enum). hint_string = "%d/%d:Zero,One,Three:3,Six:6" % [TYPE_INT, PROPERTY_HINT_ENUM] # Array of integers (an enum). hint_string = "%d/%d:*.png" % [TYPE_STRING, PROPERTY_HINT_FILE] # Array of strings (file paths). hint_string = "%d/%d:Texture2D" % [TYPE_OBJECT, PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE] # Array of textures. hint_string = "%d:%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_FLOAT] # Two-dimensional array of floats. hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_STRING, PROPERTY_HINT_MULTILINE_TEXT] # Two-dimensional array of multiline strings. hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:-1,1,0.1" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_FLOAT, PROPERTY_HINT_RANGE] # Two-dimensional array of floats (in range from -1 to 1). hint_string = "%d:%d/%d:Texture2D" % [TYPE_ARRAY, TYPE_OBJECT, PROPERTY_HINT_RESOURCE_TYPE] # Two-dimensional array of textures. [/gdscript] [csharp] hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Range:D}:1,10,1"; // Array of integers (in range from 1 to 10). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Enum:D}:Zero,One,Two"; // Array of integers (an enum). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Int:D}/{PropertyHint.Enum:D}:Zero,One,Three:3,Six:6"; // Array of integers (an enum). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.String:D}/{PropertyHint.File:D}:*.png"; // Array of strings (file paths). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Object:D}/{PropertyHint.ResourceType:D}:Texture2D"; // Array of textures. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Float:D}:"; // Two-dimensional array of floats. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.String:D}/{PropertyHint.MultilineText:D}:"; // Two-dimensional array of multiline strings. hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Float:D}/{PropertyHint.Range:D}:-1,1,0.1"; // Two-dimensional array of floats (in range from -1 to 1). hintString = $"{Variant.Type.Array:D}:{Variant.Type.Object:D}/{PropertyHint.ResourceType:D}:Texture2D"; // Two-dimensional array of textures. [/csharp] [/codeblocks] [b]Note:[/b] The trailing colon is required for properly detecting built-in types.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_OBJECT_TOO_BIG
constants.summary: Hints that an object is too big to be sent via the debugger.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NODE_PATH_VALID_TYPES
constants.summary: Hints that the hint string specifies valid node types for property of type [NodePath].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_SAVE_FILE
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path for the file to be saved at. The dialog has access to the project's directory. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. See also [member FileDialog.filters].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GLOBAL_SAVE_FILE
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] property is a path to a file. Editing it will show a file dialog for picking the path for the file to be saved at. The dialog has access to the entire filesystem. The hint string can be a set of filters with wildcards like [code]"*.png,*.jpg"[/code]. See also [member FileDialog.filters].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_INT_IS_POINTER
constants.summary: Hints that an [int] property is a pointer. Used by GDExtension.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ARRAY_TYPE
constants.summary: Hints that a property is an [Array] with the stored type specified in the hint string. The hint string contains the type of the array (e.g. [code]"String"[/code]). Use the hint string format from [constant PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING] for more control over the stored type.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_DICTIONARY_TYPE
constants.summary: Hints that a property is a [Dictionary] with the stored types specified in the hint string. The hint string contains the key and value types separated by a semicolon (e.g. [code]"int;String"[/code]). Use the hint string format from [constant PROPERTY_HINT_TYPE_STRING] for more control over the stored types.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LOCALE_ID
constants.summary: Hints that a string property is a locale code. Editing it will show a locale dialog for picking language and country.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_LOCALIZABLE_STRING
constants.summary: Hints that a dictionary property is string translation map. Dictionary keys are locale codes and, values are translated strings.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_NODE_TYPE
constants.summary: Hints that a property is an instance of a [Node]-derived type, optionally specified via the hint string (e.g. [code]"Node2D"[/code]). Editing it will show a dialog for picking a node from the scene.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_HIDE_QUATERNION_EDIT
constants.summary: Hints that a quaternion property should disable the temporary euler editor.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_PASSWORD
constants.summary: Hints that a string property is a password, and every character is replaced with the secret character.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_TOOL_BUTTON
constants.summary: Hints that a [Callable] property should be displayed as a clickable button. When the button is pressed, the callable is called. The hint string specifies the button text and optionally an icon from the [code]"EditorIcons"[/code] theme type. [codeblock lang=text] "Click me!" - A button with the text "Click me!" and the default "Callable" icon. "Click me!,ColorRect" - A button with the text "Click me!" and the "ColorRect" icon. [/codeblock] [b]Note:[/b] A [Callable] cannot be properly serialized and stored in a file, so it is recommended to use [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR] instead of [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_DEFAULT].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_ONESHOT
constants.summary: Hints that a property will be changed on its own after setting, such as [member AudioStreamPlayer.playing] or [member GPUParticles3D.emitting].

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_GROUP_ENABLE
constants.summary: Hints that a boolean property will enable the feature associated with the group that it occurs in. The property will be displayed as a checkbox on the group header. Only works within a group or subgroup. By default, disabling the property hides all properties in the group. Use the optional hint string [code]"checkbox_only"[/code] to disable this behavior.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_INPUT_NAME
constants.summary: Hints that a [String] or [StringName] property is the name of an input action. This allows the selection of any action name from the Input Map in the Project Settings. The hint string may contain two options separated by commas: - If it contains [code]"show_builtin"[/code], built-in input actions are included in the selection. - If it contains [code]"loose_mode"[/code], loose mode is enabled. This allows inserting any action name even if it's not present in the input map.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_FILE_PATH
constants.summary: Like [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE], but the property is stored as a raw path, not UID. That means the reference will be broken if you move the file. Consider using [constant PROPERTY_HINT_FILE] when possible.

constants.name: PROPERTY_HINT_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PropertyHint] enum.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NONE
constants.summary: The property is not stored, and does not display in the editor. This is the default for non-exported properties.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_STORAGE
constants.summary: The property is serialized and saved in the scene file (default for exported properties).

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR
constants.summary: The property is shown in the [EditorInspector] (default for exported properties).

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_INTERNAL
constants.summary: The property is excluded from the class reference.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CHECKABLE
constants.summary: The property can be checked in the [EditorInspector].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CHECKED
constants.summary: The property is checked in the [EditorInspector].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_GROUP
constants.summary: Used to group properties together in the editor. See [EditorInspector].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CATEGORY
constants.summary: Used to categorize properties together in the editor.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SUBGROUP
constants.summary: Used to group properties together in the editor in a subgroup (under a group). See [EditorInspector].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CLASS_IS_BITFIELD
constants.summary: The property is a bitfield, i.e. it contains multiple flags represented as bits.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NO_INSTANCE_STATE
constants.summary: The property does not save its state in [PackedScene].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_RESTART_IF_CHANGED
constants.summary: Editing the property prompts the user for restarting the editor.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE
constants.summary: The property is a script variable. [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE] can be used to distinguish between exported script variables from built-in variables (which don't have this usage flag). By default, [constant PROPERTY_USAGE_SCRIPT_VARIABLE] is [b]not[/b] applied to variables that are created by overriding [method Object._get_property_list] in a script.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_STORE_IF_NULL
constants.summary: The property value of type [Object] will be stored even if its value is [code]null[/code].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_UPDATE_ALL_IF_MODIFIED
constants.summary: If this property is modified, all inspector fields will be refreshed.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_CLASS_IS_ENUM
constants.summary: The property is a variable of enum type, i.e. it only takes named integer constants from its associated enumeration.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NIL_IS_VARIANT
constants.summary: If property has [code]nil[/code] as default value, its type will be [Variant].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_ARRAY
constants.summary: The property is the element count of a property array, i.e. a list of groups of related properties. Properties defined with this usage also need a specific [code]class_name[/code] field in the form of [code]label,prefix[/code]. The field may also include additional comma-separated options: - [code]page_size=N[/code]: Overrides [member EditorSettings.interface/inspector/max_array_dictionary_items_per_page] for this array. - [code]add_button_text=text[/code]: The text displayed by the "Add Element" button. - [code]static[/code]: The elements can't be re-arranged. - [code]const[/code]: New elements can't be added. - [code]numbered[/code]: An index will appear next to each element. - [code]unfoldable[/code]: The array can't be folded. - [code]swap_method=method_name[/code]: The method that will be called when two elements switch places. The method should take 2 [int] parameters, which will be indices of the elements being swapped. Note that making a full-fledged property array requires boilerplate code involving [method Object._get_property_list].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_ALWAYS_DUPLICATE
constants.summary: When duplicating a resource with [method Resource.duplicate], and this flag is set on a property of that resource, the property should always be duplicated, regardless of the [code]subresources[/code] bool parameter.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NEVER_DUPLICATE
constants.summary: When duplicating a resource with [method Resource.duplicate], and this flag is set on a property of that resource, the property should never be duplicated, regardless of the [code]subresources[/code] bool parameter.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_HIGH_END_GFX
constants.summary: The property is only shown in the editor if modern renderers are supported (the Compatibility rendering method is excluded).

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NODE_PATH_FROM_SCENE_ROOT
constants.summary: The [NodePath] property will always be relative to the scene's root. Mostly useful for local resources.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_RESOURCE_NOT_PERSISTENT
constants.summary: Use when a resource is created on the fly, i.e. the getter will always return a different instance. [ResourceSaver] needs this information to properly save such resources.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_KEYING_INCREMENTS
constants.summary: Inserting an animation key frame of this property will automatically increment the value, allowing to easily keyframe multiple values in a row.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR_INSTANTIATE_OBJECT
constants.summary: When this property is a [Resource] and base object is a [Node], a resource instance will be automatically created whenever the node is created in the editor.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_EDITOR_BASIC_SETTING
constants.summary: The property is considered a basic setting and will appear even when advanced mode is disabled. Used for project settings.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_READ_ONLY
constants.summary: The property is read-only in the [EditorInspector].

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_SECRET
constants.summary: An export preset property with this flag contains confidential information and is stored separately from the rest of the export preset configuration.

constants.name: PROPERTY_USAGE_NO_EDITOR
constants.summary: Default usage but without showing the property in the editor (storage).

constants.name: METHOD_FLAG_VARARG
constants.summary: Flag for a method with a variable number of arguments.

constants.name: METHOD_FLAG_OBJECT_CORE
constants.summary: Used internally. Allows to not dump core virtual methods (such as [method Object._notification]) to the JSON API.

constants.name: METHOD_FLAG_VIRTUAL_REQUIRED
constants.summary: Flag for a virtual method that is required. In GDScript, this flag is set for abstract functions.

constants.name: UINT8_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of an 8-bit unsigned integer.

constants.name: UINT16_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of a 16-bit unsigned integer.

constants.name: UINT32_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of a 32-bit unsigned integer.

constants.name: INT8_MIN
constants.summary: Minimum value of an 8-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT8_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of an 8-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT16_MIN
constants.summary: Minimum value of a 16-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT16_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of a 16-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT32_MIN
constants.summary: Minimum value of a 32-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT32_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of a 32-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT64_MIN
constants.summary: Minimum value of a 64-bit signed integer.

constants.name: INT64_MAX
constants.summary: Maximum value of a 64-bit signed integer.

constants.name: TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Variant.Type] enum.

constants.name: OP_XOR
constants.summary: Logical XOR operator (not implemented in GDScript).

constants.name: OP_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum Variant.Operator] enum.
